---
title: "Matching and Weighting Learning Resources"
date: 9 March 2026
bibliography: references.bib
---

## What This Website Is For

This website is a training and reference space for design-stage causal inference using matching and weighting methods in observational evaluation.

Its core goal is to show how to design, diagnose, and estimate observational comparisons responsibly using:

- exact matching
- coarsened exact matching
- entropy balancing for weighting

The material is organized around a practical evaluator workflow rather than a software-first workflow. The emphasis throughout the site is that:

- matching and weighting are design tools, not substitutes for causal assumptions
- balance must be assessed together with retained sample size and overlap
- entropy balancing should be treated as a weighting method, not as a matching method

The intended audience is evaluators, analysts, and staff who need a practical introduction to quasi-experimental design choices in programme and policy evaluation. The framing is especially aligned with training and capacity-building use cases, where methods need to be technically sound, explainable, and auditable.

## How Codex Was Used In This Repository

For transparency, this repository records the provider, tool, model version, role in the workflow, and the date of this disclosure. Codex is OpenAI's coding agent and software-engineering assistant, while ChatGPT is OpenAI's general-purpose conversational interface [@openai_codex; @openai_chatgpt].

In this repository, those tools were used as coding and drafting assistants rather than as autonomous authors. They helped with implementation tasks, restructuring content, generating draft text, and supporting documentation and QA work across the site. Most AI-assisted work was completed mainly with ChatGPT `GPT-5.4` [@openai_gpt54] and with Codex models `GPT-5.3-Codex` and `GPT-5.2-Codex` [@openai_gpt53codex; @openai_gpt52codex]. This disclosure was updated on 7 March 2026.

The human analyst remained responsible for the substantive direction of the project. The analyst planned the work, delegated bounded tasks to Codex, reviewed outputs, iterated drafts, checked technical and editorial quality, and decided what to keep, revise, or reject.

This means the repository should be read as analyst-directed work supported by AI assistance, not as an autonomous AI-produced resource.

## How The Site Is Structured

The site is split into three main areas:

- `Articles`: longer methods write-ups and their supporting audit materials
- `Labs`: one report-based walkthrough plus worked examples that extend the core material to other datasets and teaching scenarios
- `Slides`: one delivery deck plus standalone facilitator notes for the workshop and lab handoff

If you are new to the site, the recommended sequence is:

1. Start with the main report.
2. Use the full-code and QA pages when you want implementation detail or audit traceability.
3. Use the workshop deck and speaking notes when you want the delivery-ready teaching version.
4. Move into the LaLonde main report lab first, then the other labs as extensions.

## Articles

The main report bundle is:

- [Matching Methods Report](articles/matching_methods_report.qmd)
- [Synthesis Report: Main Report versus *The Mixtape*, *The Effect*, and Gertler Chapter 8](articles/matching_synthesis_report.qmd)
- [Propensity-Score Weighting Companion](articles/propensity_score_weighting_companion.qmd)
- [Notes on *The Effect*: Matching](articles/effectbook_matching_notes.qmd)
- [Notes on *The Mixtape*: Matching and Subclassification](articles/mixtape_matching_subclassification_notes.qmd)
- [Notes on Gertler et al.: Matching](articles/gertler_matching_notes.qmd)
- [Full Code Companion](articles/matching_methods_report_full_code.qmd)
- [QA Log](articles/matching_methods_report_qa_log.qmd)

## Labs

The lab section provides one walkthrough that stays on the main report dataset, followed by additional teaching labs that apply the same design-first logic to other examples:

- [Lab Hub](labs/index.qmd)
- [LaLonde Main Report Lab](labs/lalonde_main_report_lab.qmd)
- [Black Politicians Lab](labs/black_politicians_lab.qmd)
- [HISP IE in Practice Lab](labs/hisp_ie_practice_lab.qmd)
- [NSW + CPS Benchmark Lab](labs/nsw_cps_benchmark_lab.qmd)
- [Titanic Teaching Lab](labs/titanic_teaching_lab.qmd)

## Slides Space

A dedicated slides section now supports the full `60` minute workshop flow with one deck and one standalone notes page:

- [Slides Hub](slides/index.qmd)
- [Matching and Weighting Workshop Deck](slides/matching_workshop.qmd)
- [Workshop Speaking Notes](slides/matching_workshop_notes.qmd)
